cite: Zotpri LLC v. Burton, 2025 NY Slip Op 51956(U), 87 Misc 3d 1249(A) (Civ. Ct., Bronx County 2025)
court: Civ. Ct., Bronx County (Hon. Diane E. Lutwak)
summary: Nonpayment. Tenant's motion for leave to file an amended answer (CPLR 3025(b)) GRANTED as to payment and warranty-of-habitability defenses and counterclaims (abatement, order to correct, attorneys' fees): the landlord could show no surprise or prejudice because the pro se answer already raised conditions and payment, and once counsel is retained a tenant should be permitted to amend to get the full benefit of representation (collecting 3225 Holdings v. Imeraj; 699 Venture v. Zuniga). The LACHES defense was STRICKEN as palpably insufficient — pleading only delay (arrears reaching back two years) without the other elements (tenant's lack of knowledge/notice that the landlord would sue, and prejudice) fails (R.M.H. Estates v. Hampshire).

cite: 2075 Creston Ave. Realty LLC v. Colon, 2025 NY Slip Op 35141(U) (Civ. Ct., Bronx County 2025)
court: Civ. Ct., Bronx County (Hon. Agata E. Rumprecht-Behrens)
summary: Nonpayment. Tenant granted leave to amend (CPLR 3025(b)) and ample-need discovery (CPLR 408; Farkas) on a rent-overcharge claim. Holding of note: for a tenancy commencing AFTER HSTPA, Regina Metro's retroactivity bar does not apply; under West Pierre Assoc. LLC v. Harvey (1st Dept 2025) and the Rent Stabilization Code amendments, a tenant need only raise an issue that the rent history is UNRELIABLE to obtain discovery — here a certified DHCR history showing an unexplained ~$900 between-tenant increase plus DOB records showing no permitted work sufficed. The landlord's reliance on 1990s cases and a 'fraudulent scheme' standard was misplaced. Discovery demands 1-8 granted; demand #9 (all units 2016-18) stricken as overbroad.

cite: BSP GIGI LLC v. Dixon, 2026 NY Slip Op 30008(U) (Civ. Ct., Bronx County 2026)
court: Civ. Ct., Bronx County (Hon. Agata E. Rumprecht-Behrens)
summary: Nonpayment proceeding DISMISSED for a defective predicate notice under the CARES Act. Where the building has a federally backed multifamily mortgage (15 USC 9058) — including a mortgage that originated with a private lender but was later assigned to Fannie Mae — the landlord must serve a 30-day notice, not the 14-day rent demand. The statute's 'purchased or securitized' language has no exception for privately originated loans later acquired by a federally backed lender. A defective predicate notice cannot be amended (Chinatown), requiring dismissal. Collects Belplain Realty Co. Inc. v. Caro, 85 Misc 3d 1278(A) (Civ. Ct. Bronx 2025) and Andrews Plaza Hous. Assoc. LP v. Rodriguez.

cite: Riverdale HM LLC v. Weissbart, 2026 NY Slip Op 30001(U) (Civ. Ct., Bronx County 2026)
court: Civ. Ct., Bronx County (Hon. Rina Gurung)
summary: Rent-stabilized nuisance holdover settled by a two-attorney stipulation imposing a 12-month probationary period. The landlord's motion to restore directly for a final judgment of possession and warrant was DENIED. The stipulation, construed by its plain language as a contract (Banos v. Rhea, 25 NY3d 266), unambiguously required the landlord to first move for a HEARING on whether a material breach occurred, and to support that motion with an affidavit from a person with personal knowledge stating the dates, times, and locations of the alleged breach. The landlord skipped the hearing step and its affidavit lacked those specifics, so the motion was denied in its entirety.
